file_insights.purpose: Core event bus service using EventEmitter2 for cross-module communication
key_exports: Multiple domain event payload interfaces (Menu, Category, Product, Order, User, Organization, Subscription, Module, Notification, Media)
primary_patterns: Type-safe event system using generic types
Statistics tracking for emitted events and listeners
Error handling with dedicated error handler
Both async and sync emission modes

patterns_discovered.description: Uses TypeScript generics to provide type safety for event payloads through a DomainEvents interface mapping event names to their payload types
patterns_discovered.location: DomainEvents interface and emit/on generic methods
patterns_discovered.benefit: Compile-time type checking prevents payload mismatches between emitters and listeners

patterns_discovered.description: Automatically adds timestamp to all event payloads during emission
patterns_discovered.benefit: Ensures all events have temporal context without requiring manual addition

patterns_discovered.description: Provides both async (emitAsync) and sync (emit) methods for events
patterns_discovered.benefit: Allows fire-and-forget patterns for non-critical events while supporting await patterns for critical ones

patterns_discovered.description: Events use dot notation (e.g., 'menu.created', 'user.login') with wildcard listener support
patterns_discovered.location: EventEmitter2 configuration with wildcard and delimiter settings
patterns_discovered.benefit: Enables efficient bulk listening to related events via wildcard patterns (e.g., 'menu.*')

patterns_discovered.description: Maintains counters for total events emitted and active listeners per event
patterns_discovered.benefit: Enables monitoring and debugging of event system health

patterns_discovered.description: Event handlers return unsubscribe functions for cleanup
patterns_discovered.benefit: Prevents memory leaks and allows dynamic subscription management

patterns_discovered.description: emitCustom() and onCustom() methods for non-domain events
patterns_discovered.benefit: Allows modules to create domain-specific events beyond the core DomainEvents map

gotchas_discovered.description: If a payload already contains a timestamp, it will be overwritten with the current time during emission
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Pass timestamp-less payloads or use metadata field for original timestamps if needed

gotchas_discovered.description: EventEmitter2 is configured with maxListeners: 20, which will trigger warnings if exceeded
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Monitor listener growth; consider refactoring if single event exceeds 20 listeners

gotchas_discovered.description: ignoreErrors is set to false, meaning listener errors will propagate but emit() catches them via promise rejection
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Ensure all async listeners have try-catch blocks; monitor emitter 'error' event

gotchas_discovered.description: Wildcard listeners (e.g., 'menu.*') will be triggered for every matching event, potentially impacting performance with many events
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Use sparingly; avoid wildcard listeners in hot paths; consider namespacing strategies

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Type safety limitations with custom events
gotchas_discovered.description: emitCustom() and onCustom() bypass type checking and accept any string event name
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Document custom events externally; consider extending DomainEvents for frequently-used custom events

approach_outcome.summary: Successfully created a comprehensive, production-ready event bus service
key_accomplishments: Implemented type-safe event system with 25+ domain event types covering all major application domains
Integrated EventEmitter2 with optimized configuration for enterprise use
Provided dual async/sync emission modes for flexible event handling
Built-in statistics tracking for monitoring and debugging
Comprehensive documentation with examples for all public methods
Support for both typed domain events and untyped custom events
Proper error handling with dedicated error callback
Memory-safe subscription management with unsubscribe functions
approach_outcome.test_coverage: No tests visible in diff, but service design supports testability with injectable decorator and clear method contracts
approach_outcome.code_quality: High - extensive JSDoc comments, clear type definitions, consistent naming conventions, proper separation of concerns

recommendations.recommendation: Create comprehensive unit tests for EventService covering: typed event emission/subscription, async/sync behavior, wildcard listeners, error handling, and statistics tracking
Create a dedicated event registry document listing all domain events, their payloads, which services emit them, and which services consume them
Implement event listener health monitoring; add telemetry to track listener execution time and failure rates per event type
Consider adding event middleware/interceptors pattern for cross-cutting concerns like logging, metrics, and replay functionality
Add strict payload validation for domain events to catch invalid emissions early in development
Export EventStats via a dedicated metric endpoint for production monitoring; implement alerts for maxListeners threshold breaches
Document the event schema versioning strategy for handling event payload evolution in the future
